overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly active and professional presence across academic-focused niches on LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, totaling 3,580 followers. His content is highly focused on computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. Across all platforms, he enjoys a strong and highly interactive audience, with discussions around newly published tools, papers, and career opportunities driving the most consistent engagement.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research papers, methodology discussions, and presentation announcements focused on identifying coordinated campaigns across platforms.
Announcements regarding tool releases, such as CooRnet v2.0, that improve algorithmic detection of coordinated link sharing.
Promoting career and research funding opportunities, including postdoctoral openings, PhD opportunities, and newly awarded research grants.

recommendations: Leverage the larger audience base on LinkedIn to cross-promote academic tools like CooRnet, which currently perform well on smaller platforms like Mastodon but could gain wider adoption on professional networks.
Increase posting frequency on Bluesky to capitalize on its high engagement rate (averaging over 6%), particularly for real-time discussion and recruiting.
Utilize rich media, such as screenshots of tools or figures from papers, to further drive visual engagement, especially for complex methodological discussions.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows the highest overall engagement rate (averaging 6.14% per post), driven heavily by a highly responsive academic community interested in computational social science and research opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides niche, high-quality engagement for specialized tool updates (like CooRnet v2.0), making it ideal for technical outreach despite its smaller follower count.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn serves as the primary audience hub with 1,850 followers and generates the highest absolute engagement volume, particularly for major milestones like research grants and hiring announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
